title: Add Social Share Buttons Using Sharer.js Library
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want social sharing buttons for Twitter, Facebook, LinkedIn, and Email in the Share modal, so that I can easily promote DataTools Pro to my professional network.
description:
Adds functional social sharing buttons to the left pane of the Share popup modal (see MBT-341). Uses the sharer.js library hosted locally to avoid CSP issues encountered with ShareThis.

## Implementation
- Download sharer.js from [ellisonleao/sharer.js](https://ellisonleao.github.io/sharer.js/) and host on the application server
- Create buttons for: Twitter, Facebook, LinkedIn, Email
- Buttons arranged two per row with the social media platform name
- Each platform uses a unique tracked URL with analytics parameters:
  - Twitter, Facebook, LinkedIn: TinyURL-wrapped tracking links
  - Email: Links to the DataTools for Salesforce landing page

## Styling
- Button text styled with white color for contrast against colored backgrounds
acceptance_criteria: Share modal left pane displays buttons for Twitter, Facebook, LinkedIn, and Email
Buttons are arranged two per row with platform names
Each button opens the correct sharing flow for its platform
Twitter, Facebook, and LinkedIn buttons use tracked URLs with analytics parameters
Email button links to the DataTools for Salesforce landing page
Button text is white for proper contrast
Sharer.js is hosted locally on the application server
ux_changes: Adds four social sharing buttons (Twitter, Facebook, LinkedIn, Email) to the left pane of the Share modal. Buttons are styled with white text on colored backgrounds, arranged in a 2-column layout.
implementation_notes: Used sharer.js (open-source library) hosted locally instead of ShareThis CDN to comply with CSP headers. Each platform button uses tracked URLs — Twitter/Facebook/LinkedIn use TinyURL-wrapped tracking links, Email uses the product landing page URL. Initially shared app.datatoolspro.com, updated to use platform-specific tracked URLs per product owner.
comment_summary: Developer implemented sharing buttons and initially used app.datatoolspro.com as the shared URL. Product owner provided platform-specific tracked URLs (via TinyURL) for analytics and requested white button text CSS. Developer updated links and styling promptly.
